text.title: Grafana Dashboard Provisioning
text.summary: Dashboard-as-code via ConfigMap, GitOps pattern, and the dashboard maturity model.

rule.statement: Provision all production dashboards as Kubernetes ConfigMaps with the grafana_dashboard=1 label. Never rely on UI-created dashboards in production.
rule.rationale: UI dashboards are lost on pod restart or cluster recreation. ConfigMap-provisioned dashboards are version-controlled, reviewed in PRs, and survive infra events. The label triggers automatic discovery by Grafana's sidecar.

example.code: apiVersion: v1 kind: ConfigMap metadata: name: my-service-dashboard namespace: monitoring labels: grafana_dashboard: "1" data: my-service.json: | { "title": "My Service", "uid": "my-service-v1", "schemaVersion": 38, "version": 1, "panels": [] }
example.note: Set a stable uid in the dashboard JSON. UIDs are used in links between dashboards and must not change after initial provisioning.

rule.statement: Configure Grafana to load dashboards from a folder mounted via the sidecar. Set allowUiUpdates: false in production to prevent UI drift from the Git-managed source.
rule.rationale: allowUiUpdates: true allows operators to save UI changes that are then lost on the next pod restart (they are not written back to the ConfigMap). Disabling it enforces the GitOps workflow.

example.code: # grafana-provisioning/dashboards/default.yaml apiVersion: 1 providers: - name: default orgId: 1 type: file disableDeletion: false allowUiUpdates: false options: path: /var/lib/grafana/dashboards foldersFromFilesStructure: true

section.title: Dashboard Maturity Model
section.p: Move teams from Low to High maturity deliberately — do not attempt to jump directly to High without foundations in place.

list.item: Low: ad-hoc creation in UI, no variables, hard-coded data source names, no version control. Dashboard sprawl; nobody knows which dashboard is authoritative.
Medium: variables for namespace/pod, consistent naming conventions, dashboards organized by service/team, basic GitOps (manual export to Git). Reliable for known services.

High: all dashboards provisioned via GitOps, shared template dashboards for new services, usage tracking to retire stale dashboards, automated dashboard generation from service metadata. Dashboards survive cluster rebuilds.

rule.statement: Add deployment and alert annotations to all service dashboards. Annotations correlate metric changes with the events that caused them.
rule.rationale: Without deployment markers, engineers spend minutes correlating a latency spike to the release that caused it. Annotations make the correlation instant and reduce MTTR.
